analysis.summary: The previous fix (narrowing Footer.tsx's useEffect dependency to directChildrenKey) was confirmed to already be in place and did NOT resolve the error. The root cause must be specific to FooterInfoSocialItem, not to Footer.tsx. After exhaustive comparison with the working FooterNavColumnItem and FooterCreditsItem blocks, the critical differences are: (1) FooterInfoSocialItem uses __experimentalLinkControl directly instead of the @taktdev/components Link component, (2) it uses the IconPicker component which renders BlockControls + MediaUploadCheck + MediaUpload on every render, and (3) it has logoId and iconSlug attributes without defaults. The error 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading meta)' in editEntityRecord's Array.reduce occurs when WordPress tries to merge edits into an entity record where the meta property is undefined. In the site editor (template part editing), this can be triggered when components cause store operations during the block insertion lifecycle before the entity record is fully stabilized.
analysis.rootCause: FooterInfoSocialItem is the ONLY footer item block that renders MediaUpload + MediaUploadCheck + two separate BlockControls fills on mount (one from the inline link toolbar, one from IconPicker). This heavier component tree causes additional WordPress store operations during the insertion lifecycle. Specifically, MediaUploadCheck calls canUser('create','media') which queries core-data, and the dual BlockControls registration triggers block toolbar slot-fill reconciliation. These extra store operations during the insertion transaction can cause editEntityRecord to be called for the template part entity at a moment when the entity record's meta field is not yet populated. The working blocks (FooterNavColumnItem, FooterCreditsItem) use the lighter-weight @taktdev/components Link component and have no MediaUpload/IconPicker on initial render, avoiding these store interactions during insertion.

previousFixAttempt.description: Narrowed Footer.tsx useEffect dependency from full getBlocks() tree to directChildrenKey string
previousFixAttempt.result: FAILED - error persists because the root cause is not in Footer.tsx's useEffect but in FooterInfoSocialItem's component tree
previousFixAttempt.note: The directChildrenKey optimization is still a valid performance improvement and should be kept

FooterNavColumnItem.blockControls: none (Link component handles its own popover)

FooterInfoSocialItem.mediaUpload: yes (from IconPicker, renders on every mount)

changePlan.strategy: Refactor FooterInfoSocialItem to match the pattern of working footer item blocks: use the shared @taktdev/components Link component for link editing, and defer IconPicker initialization. This reduces the number of store-interacting components that mount during block insertion.

changes.description: Replace direct __experimentalLinkControl usage with the shared @taktdev/components Link component. This matches the pattern used by FooterNavColumnItem and FooterCreditsItem, which do NOT have this error. The Link component wraps LinkControl with safe guards and handles postId/postType mapping correctly.
changes.details: Remove the imports for __experimentalLinkControl, Popover, ToggleControl, TextControl, useState, useRef from WordPress. Import { Link } from @taktdev/components instead. Replace the manual link toolbar + popover with the Link component's built-in popover. Keep the IconPicker integration inside the Link's children prop.

changes.description: Defer IconPicker rendering until the block is selected/focused. This prevents MediaUploadCheck and MediaUpload from mounting during the insertion lifecycle, avoiding store operations that trigger editEntityRecord before the entity record is ready.
changes.details: Import useSelect from @wordpress/data and check isBlockSelected or hasSelectedInnerBlock. Only render IconPicker (with its MediaUpload) when the block is selected. When not selected, render a static icon display (if icon is set) or a placeholder. This eliminates MediaUpload from the insertion-time component tree.

changes.description: Remove the explicit BlockControls for the link toolbar since the Link component handles this internally. The IconPicker's BlockControls should remain but only render when the block is selected (per CHANGE-002).
changes.details: The current code renders two BlockControls fills. Reducing to zero on initial mount (Link handles its own UI, IconPicker deferred) eliminates the slot-fill reconciliation overhead during insertion.

implementation.approach: Rewrite the Edit component to use the Link component pattern from @taktdev/components, wrapping the IconPicker as children of the Link. Defer IconPicker until block is selected.
implementation.referencePattern: Follow the exact pattern from FooterNavColumnItem.tsx and FooterCreditsItem.tsx for the link handling. For the icon display, render a static SVG/image when not selected, and the full IconPicker when selected.
removedImports: ToolbarGroup, ToolbarButton, Popover, ToggleControl, TextControl from @wordpress/components
codeStructure: 1. Keep suggestedIcons array and SVG imports as-is
a. Compute hasIcon and iconPickerValue as before
b. Use useSelect to check if block is selected
d. Inside <li>, render Link component with:
- onChange handler that merges link, calls renameBlock, and setAttributes
- children: conditional IconPicker (if selected) or static icon display (if not selected)
e. IconPicker onChange sets iconSlug/logoId via setAttributes
3. Link component handles its own popover, toolbar, and link editing

changes.description: Add explicit defaults for logoId and iconSlug attributes to prevent undefined values during block creation. While this alone may not fix the error, it ensures cleaner block serialization.
changes.details: Set logoId default to null (or 0) and iconSlug default to empty string. This prevents undefined attribute values during block creation, which could contribute to edge cases in WordPress's block serialization.

alternativeFixes.approach: Minimal fix: Only defer the IconPicker rendering without changing the Link component
alternativeFixes.description: Keep __experimentalLinkControl but wrap the entire UI in a check for block selection state. When the block is first inserted (not yet selected), render only a minimal <li> placeholder. When selected, render the full UI with LinkControl and IconPicker.
alternativeFixes.pros: Smaller diff, keeps existing link editing behavior
alternativeFixes.cons: Still uses __experimentalLinkControl (divergent from other footer blocks), doesn't address the root pattern inconsistency

alternativeFixes.approach: Try-catch wrapper: Wrap the block edit in an ErrorBoundary
alternativeFixes.description: Add a React ErrorBoundary around the Edit component that catches the editEntityRecord error and gracefully handles it
alternativeFixes.pros: Doesn't require refactoring, prevents the error from being uncaught
alternativeFixes.cons: Masks the root cause rather than fixing it, error still occurs silently, doesn't prevent potential data loss during the failed editEntityRecord

steps: 1. Open the site editor and navigate to the Footer template part
2. Find the FooterInfoSocial section with existing social items
3. Open the browser console (F12) and clear it
4. Click the Appender (+) button to add a new FooterInfoSocialItem
5. VERIFY: No console error appears (previously: 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading meta)')
6. VERIFY: The new social item is added successfully and appears in the list
7. Click on the new social item to select it
8. VERIFY: The link editing popover opens correctly (via the Link component)
9. Set a URL and title for the social link
10. VERIFY: renameBlock works (block name updates in list view)
11. Click the Icon toolbar button and select a suggested icon (e.g., Facebook)
12. VERIFY: Icon renders correctly in the block
13. Add a second social item and verify no errors on insertion
14. Save the template part and reload -- verify all items persist with correct icons and links
15. Open a page that uses the footer template part in the front end -- verify social links render
regressionChecks: Existing social items still display their icons correctly
Link editing still works for existing items (URL, title, aria label, new tab toggle)
Custom icon upload via media library still works
FooterNavColumnItem Appender still works without errors
FooterCreditsItem Appender still works without errors
Footer direct children enforcement (from Footer.tsx useEffect) still works

decisions.decision: Replace __experimentalLinkControl with @taktdev/components Link to match working footer item blocks
decisions.evidence: FooterNavColumnItem and FooterCreditsItem use @taktdev/components Link and have no insertion error. FooterInfoSocialItem uses __experimentalLinkControl directly and has the error. Using the shared component aligns with the working pattern.

decisions.decision: Defer IconPicker rendering until block is selected to reduce insertion-time store operations
decisions.evidence: IconPicker renders MediaUploadCheck + MediaUpload on every mount, triggering core-data store queries (canUser) during the block insertion lifecycle. Other footer items have no MediaUpload and no insertion error.

decisions.decision: Add explicit defaults for logoId and iconSlug in block.json
decisions.evidence: Attributes without defaults get undefined during block creation. While FooterNavColumnItem also lacks extra attributes, having undefined values during insertion could contribute to serialization edge cases.

decisions.decision: Keep the directChildrenKey optimization in Footer.tsx from the previous fix
decisions.evidence: The optimization is valid for performance even though it did not fix this specific bug. It prevents unnecessary useEffect runs on nested block changes.
